text: Neurotransmitters and their receptors are essential for brain functioning, learning, and memory.  Catecholamines, including dopamine and norepinephrine, are the main neurotransmitters that mediate a variety of central nervous system (CNS) functions, such as motor control, cognition, emotion, memory processing, and endocrine modulation, determined by recent molecular genetic approaches in mice (Handra et al., 2019).
